prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT medium-sized object standing FREE in empty space, resting on a small flat patch of clear blue water; the object rises straight up and IS the sprite. CRITICAL: the entire background is FULLY TRANSPARENT and there is NO black outline, NO dark border, NO frame, NO box, NO rectangle, NO square, NO cube edges and NO wireframe anywhere around the tile or the object — the object's silhouette is soft and organic against empty transparency, with no containing lines or hard straight edges enclosing it. Do NOT stand it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a raised cube: just a small low patch at its base. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a stone lantern on a rock 2) a mossy standing stone in the water 3) a leaning driftwood snag 4) a rock topped with a gnarled shrub 5) a cluster of tall bulrushes 6) a wooden rowboat moored to a post 7) a small rock islet with a bush 8) a bare dead tree rising from the water 9) a tall stand of reeds and cattails 10) a tall clump of papyrus. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
